intro: Limonium × abnorme, also known as Sea Lavender or Abnormal Sea Lavender, is a hybrid plant species within the Plumbaginaceae family, characterized by its distinctive, fragrant flowers and succulent leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Limonium × abnorme include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
